meaning: adj.肮脏的,不整洁的,破旧的
examples: For months, workers have been feverishly sprucing up Dili, Timor-Leste's scruffy capital.
In a scruffy Budapest district, a postgraduate student from Cambridge is getting a bruising reception.
The traffic was aggressive and choked; the pedestrian crossings deadly; the buses overcrowded; the train stations scruffy and underfunded.
The people who took part were sometimes smartly dressed and at other times scruffy.
An almost forgotten face, scruffy with stubble, ranked No. 216 in the world, improbably climbing higher.
